title: pittosporum cornifolium
intro: Pittosporum cornifolium, also known as the Kohuhu, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its dark green leaves and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pittosporum cornifolium include its ability to aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used in teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
